title: Paired analysis + committed comparison artifact + cold-reader gate
description: Paired per-task deltas, medians + sign test, publish the raw per-pair table (show distributions, no single-anecdote claims). Layout: .agent/demos/uplift-two-arm/{pairs.json, arm-runs/, metrics.csv, report.md, regenerate.sh}. Honest n caveats. Cold-reader gate before campaign closure.

title: Run the two-arm protocol (pack arm vs raw-ref arm)
description: Execute the paired protocol; both arms auto-captured by the archive itself (the instrument measures its own experiment).
design: Sampling: find_abandoned_sessions severity question_left|error_left, session.repo in {polylogue,sinnix}, 90 days, authored_user_messages>=3, exclude >2M-token sessions; N=12-20 pairs (or start n=1 with an exported devloop; continuation task 'state current slice, open threads, next action', ground truth = conductor packet). Task extraction: the unresolved question/error verbatim — identical prompt both arms. Arm A: fresh session, prompt only. Arm B: prompt + compose_context_preamble output. Same model; pin repo state to the session_commits commit via worktree. Metrics (post-hoc from archive): turns-to-first-file-edit; Read/Grep actions targeting files the preamble already cited (re-discovery waste); tool-error count; wall-clock; total tokens; terminal_state. Randomize arm order per pair; run pairs serially (cache/quota bias).

title: Campaign: handoff-pack two-arm uplift experiment
description: First true uplift measurement in either repo: does a Polylogue context pack make a continuation agent measurably better than a raw session ref? Everything finished so far proves honesty; nothing proves a stranger should care. Sequenced third per operator direction. n=1 minimum viable (the two exported 20-hour devloops as subject), n=12-20 pairs for the publishable version.

title: Regenerate handoff pack on current archive; promote to curated shelf
description: The composed `find "session:X" then read --view temporal,chronicle` handoff emits a bounded typed zero-omission pack (~773-token estimate from a 4,600+-message session). Regenerate on current archive; promote from the retired inbox shelf to .agent/demos.

title: Campaign: agent-forensics regeneration + all-provider repricing
description: Regenerate the agent-forensics packet on the current archive with an honest all-provider headline. The 2026-06-27 report (546.6B tokens, $89,368 API-list equivalent, 216x cache amplification) is the most stranger-legible artifact on any shelf, but: numbers are pre-dedup stale, and the $89,368 prices only the priced-provenance subset (Claude Code rows with cost_usd); Codex/ChatGPT/Gemini are origin_reported token counts with no dollar value (operator estimate ~$150K all-provider). Sequenced after claim-vs-evidence per operator direction 2026-07-02.

title: Rerun forensics on current archive; extend pricing to origin_reported providers
description: Rerun scripts/agent_forensics.py against the current archive (v23+); price origin_reported providers via the vendored LiteLLM catalog (match last path segment); either an all-provider headline or explicitly-labeled per-provenance figures that cannot be misread; record deltas vs the 06-27 version; verify chart SVGs render. Cache-inclusion must be disambiguated (Codex input INCLUDES cached ~96%; see bd memories on token semantics).

title: Campaign: claim-vs-evidence report to finding-grade
description:
Terminal state: an externally publishable finding ('how often do coding agents proceed past failed tool calls, by model/tool') with stated sample frame, calibrated markers, benign/consequential split, seeded stranger-runnable reproduction, and a passed cold-reader gate. Slice closure is NOT campaign closure; this epic stays top-of-frame until terminal state is recorded (.agent/conductor-devloop/PROCESS.md External-Proof Campaigns).

State as of 2026-07-03 (schema v23, 16,494 sessions): sample-frame bias fixed; 41,774 classifiable failures; Codex rows present (1,250/5,000 inspected); classification_reason + matched_marker + next_text_preview exposed in samples; regen latency 43s (see perf bead). Artifact: .agent/demos/claim-vs-evidence/claim-vs-evidence.report.json.
notes: Operator priority frame 2026-07-02: proof campaigns outrank all substrate slices.

title: Verify v23 FTS readiness end-to-end: find works; readiness is O(1) ledger read
description: v23 added fts_freshness_state + the text-populated partial index after `find hermes` refused with 'Search index is incomplete' despite healthy FTS (drift in recorded counts). Verify on the live archive: (a) polylogue find <term> works; (b) readiness hot path reads the ledger row, no recount-scan; (c) triggers maintain source_rows/indexed_rows +-1 (insert/delete/update) and the bulk trigger-suspension path recomputes exact counts once post-rebuild; (d) recount lives only in ops doctor. Fix whatever of a-d is missing; add a regression so status cannot report healthy while find refuses. Pitfall: contentless FTS delete-markers do not change the +-1 arithmetic.

title: Demo shelf claim currentness enforcement
description: devloop-refresh-demos --check must require claim/non-claim/proof/caveat fields, report unsummarized current entries, and fail on stale schema claims (an archive-debt demo cited v19 while live was v21). Enabler tier: campaign artifacts sit on this shelf; stale claims poison external citations.

title: Browser-capture embedded attachment payloads -> blob store as acquired
description: Live repro: claude-ai session 2c2eab57... has 60 attachment metadata entries, 16 with embedded extracted_content in the raw capture — but all 59 indexed attachment_refs are acquisition_status='unfetched' with empty blob_hash. Write embedded payloads to the blob store as acquired (true SHA-256); keep non-embedded upload refs as honest unfetched debt. Repro packet: /realm/inbox/curr_state/hermes-project-comparison-browser-capture (copy into repo-local fixture before relying on it; inbox is staging). Related gh-2456 (chip-only capture) and gh-2479 (non-inline acquisition) — this slice is the already-captured-bytes case.

title: Import/browser-capture freshness: newest-wins ordering; DOM fallback must not overwrite richer sessions
description: Last-writer-wins can let older GDPR/browser payloads replace newer bodies while keeping updated_at_ms=MAX(existing,incoming); DOM-fallback ChatGPT/Claude captures can overwrite richer native/GDPR sessions; same-length changed captures can be skipped by the stale raw guard. Add newest-wins tests across browser/GDPR orderings; prevent DOM fallback canonical overwrite; make normal import wait/convergence operation-scoped. Data-integrity bug: silent evidence downgrade.

title: devtools lab probe cost-reconciliation (vs state_5.sqlite + stats-cache.json)
description: Reconcile stored accounting against authoritative external stores: Codex per-thread median ratio expect 1.00; Claude lane-by-lane (disjoint lanes, never fold cache into input). Copy state_5.sqlite to scratch first (live-locked). Validates every number the campaigns publish. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: MCP scoped aggregates silently capped at page limit (wrong totals)
description: facets caps scoped buckets at limit=10; aggregate/correlate clamp to 1000 with no truncated flag; EXPECTED_TOOL_NAMES misses three tools and the contract test only checks a subset. Wrong totals on an agent-facing surface = trust bug. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Count tokens on logical-session basis (fork/resume replays double-count)
description: Child rollups still count inherited-prefix tokens: Codex live 2.12x vs authoritative state_5.sqlite (closes to ~1.08x on re-ingest); Claude +9.6%. Attribute to logical_session_id root; slice rollups to the tail; present API-equivalent vs subscription-equivalent as separate columns. ENABLER: blocks the forensics repricing campaign headline. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Session lineage model: shared-content duplication corrupts counts/cost/search
description: Fork/resume/compaction share content; storage+aggregates ignored it. v12-v14 landed prefix-dedup + composition; residuals tracked as children. Design doc docs/design/session-lineage-model.md. Operator: 'broken unless modeled correctly' — correctness > demo-ladder. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Execute lineage validation plan (gates citing archive counts externally)
description: Validation plan exists at .agent/scratch/research/12-lineage-validation.md. Operator tier frame lists this as a campaign enabler: it gates citing ANY archive count externally (forensics headline, claim-vs-evidence denominators).

title: Scale-hardening: bugs that only bite on real-scale archives
description: Confirmed-severe set of code correct on small/clean fixtures but wrong at real scale (e.g. full insight rebuild = one transaction -> 6GB WAL + minutes-long write lock). Work the checklist on the issue; tier 1 items observed live. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Audit 39,586 missing referenced blobs in production backup
description: Backup verifier warns 'referenced blobs missing: 39586'. Likely dominated by the synthetic attachment blob_hash rows (attachments metadata-only bead) — classify via ops maintenance blob-reference-debt, then split real acquisition debt from by-construction fakes. Gates trusting full_evidence backups. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: EPIC: Polylogue as local AI-work evidence cockpit
description: Umbrella epic mirroring gh#1807: Polylogue is a local evidence cockpit for the operator's own AI-work history — understanding layer (resume/digest/evidence/query/cost/assertions, realized core) + substrate direction (#2384, confirm before large build). Not a proof catalog, QA dashboard, or PM clone. Coordination only; children own code. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Seeded stranger-runnable reproduction + cold-reader gate
description: Package the finding so a fresh agent given ONLY the artifact directory can state what it proves, name sample frame and caveats, and reproduce it (seeded demo corpus path, no private data). Record the cold read as part of the demo packet. Methodology children must land first — do not package a moving measurement.

title: Sample-frame statement in the report (which N of which total, ordering, window)
description: Mostly landed 2026-07-03 (sample-frame bias fixed; 41,774 classifiable failures stated; 100 unpaired coverage gaps reported). Remaining: verify the report text states ordering/time-window and the inspected-vs-total relation a stranger can weigh; close after cold read of the current artifact.

title: Acknowledge-later sensitivity window (next 3 assistant turns)
description: Next-turn-only classification overstates silence. Add a sensitivity row using a window of the next 3 assistant turns; report both next-turn and windowed rates side by side.

title: Calibrate ack-marker precision/recall on ~50 hand-labeled follow-ups
description: The ack-marker vocabulary's recall is unproven. Hand-label ~50 failure follow-ups, report marker precision/recall next to the headline. This is the gate on quoting any silent-proceed percentage externally. Note: classification_reason/matched_marker fields already exposed in samples (07-03), which makes labeling cheap.

title: Characterize ambiguous bucket: wordless continuation vs prose-without-markers
description: Split next-turn-is-tool-call (wordless continuation) from prose-lacking-ack-markers inside the ambiguous class; state counts for both. Wordless continuation arguably belongs in silent-proceed — keeping it separate is fine but must be explicit. Opus-4-7 74% ambiguous vs deepseek 17% is likely turn-structure variance, not behavior — this split is what disambiguates.

title: Benign-recovery vs consequential-silence split by handler kind
description: Read failures are ~94% silent but 'tried another path' is usually benign; Bash/test failures are the consequential class. Scope the headline to consequential handler kinds or add an explicit benign/consequential split — credibility depends on not inflating with trivial recoveries.

title: Expose action-unit outcome fields + followup_class as product capability
description: Capabilities-may-not-be-silos gate for the campaign: the facts the report needs must become composable query capability, not bespoke table reads. After this, the whole report is `actions where is_error:true | group by session.origin, followup_class | count` and every future cut (model/tool/repo/time) is free.
design: 1) is_error/exit_code are normalized at parse time (sources/parsers/base_models.py:74-75) but ActionQueryRowPayload (surfaces/payloads.py:~1298) carries neither — add as filterable/groupable action-unit fields. 2) Add derived followup_class (acknowledged|silent_proceed|wordless_continuation|ambiguous) + followup_message_ref computed in the source-derived lowering (no cache tables). 3) Retire or reduce devtools workspace claim-vs-evidence to a render preset over these query strings. Registry/completions/render-regen chain: stage parser -> AST to_payload -> executor -> metadata.py aggregate_group_fields -> shell_completion_values.py -> devtools render openapi + cli-output-schemas + cli-reference.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture session with known unacknowledged failure fires via pure query strings; report README numbers reproducible from the printed queries.

title: Decision: beads vs assertions boundary
description:
Question (raw-log 2026-07-03): how do Polylogue assertions relate/compare to beads — colliding systems or complementary?

Analysis: they share the shape 'typed, authored, timestamped claim with lifecycle' but differ on domain and authority. Beads = prospective coordination (work not yet done): ready/blocked/closed lifecycle, dependency graph, assignees; its store is its own Dolt DB; authority = the humans/agents coordinating. Assertions = retrospective epistemics (claims about evidence that exists): candidate->judged->active lifecycle, evidence_refs into the archive, context-injection policy; authority = the judgment gate. A bead says 'someone should do X'; an assertion says 'X is true about ref Y, per evidence Z'.

Boundary rule: task/dependency/scheduling state lives in beads; knowledge, provenance, and anything that feeds agent context lives in assertions. Where they touch: (1) a closed bead's close-reason is a CLAIM — verifying it against archive evidence is claim-vs-evidence composition (see beads-ingest bead); (2) bd 'memories' overlap assertion NOTE/LESSON kinds — prefer bd memories for repo-operational lore agents need at bd prime time, assertions for archive-linked knowledge with evidence refs; (3) do NOT rebuild a task tracker inside assertions (the gh-1807 epic already forbids Bundle-as-second-task-tracker) and do NOT put evidence-linked knowledge in beads descriptions beyond pointers.

Status: adopt as working doctrine; revisit if beads-ingest lands and the seam moves.

title: Align cross-repo devloop contract on beads (Sinex parity)
description: The shared conductor conventions (includes/devloop-conventions.md) predate beads: Sinex has devloop-checkpoint --queue as the deferred-directive channel; Polylogue now uses beads as the durable directive/backlog substrate. Update the shared contract in BOTH repos: operator directives = P0/P1 beads (bd human for decisions needing the operator), QUEUE.md retired, contract-drift check extended to beads presence. Do not let the twin repos drift silently again (a --queue call was once swallowed as a checkpoint title).

title: Deepen beads integration in devloop scripts
description: Docs + devloop-status summary land with the beads migration (2026-07-03). Remaining wiring: devloop-start optionally takes a bead id and claims it (bd update --claim) recording it in ACTIVE-LOOP.md; devloop-review warns on in_progress beads with no matching active slice and on bd dep cycles; devloop-checkpoint closes/updates the slice bead; devloop-handoff includes bd ready snapshot. Keep bd optional (command -v bd guard) so the loop still runs where beads is absent.

title: SessionStart preamble opt-in rollout (polylogue + sinnix repos)
description: Wire compose_context_preamble into SessionStart hooks per-project via .claude/settings.json, starting with polylogue and sinnix repos only (operator decision). Measure before global: preamble presence is arm B of the uplift experiment; rollout and experiment reinforce each other. Keep injection restrained — indices/refs over dumps (raw-log criterion).

title: Local embedding lane via OpenAI-compatible provider (LiteLLM gateway)
description: Voyage is the only embedding provider; a local lane makes semantic search $0 and the whole loop air-gapped (also the sovereign-loop story for the Nous/local-Llama audience).
design: Seam: VectorProvider protocol + Voyage constants in sqlite_vec_support.py. Config: [embedding] provider='openai-compatible', base_url, model, dimension in polylogue.toml; implement the OpenAI /v1/embeddings client shape once (LiteLLM gateway 127.0.0.1:4000 bridges to Ollama). Dimension: vec0 table is fixed float[1024] and EMBEDDING_DIMENSION is asserted in meta CHECK — dimension becomes a tier-init parameter recorded in embeddings.db meta; changing model/dimension => ops reset --embeddings + backfill (tier is designed expensive-rebuild; NO in-place migration); bump EMBEDDINGS_SCHEMA_VERSION. Cost preflight must branch on provider ($0 local), not hardcode Voyage constants. Eval before switching default: embed demo corpus + 200 live prose messages with both models; compare --similar top-10 overlap + a 20-query hand-relevance check.

title: Reboot-with-refs: session self-compaction protocol
description: Agent reboots into a fresh session carrying all prose verbatim with every tool exchange collapsed to a one-line ref — better than harness compaction because refs are expandable via resolve_ref. Raw-log 06-29: refs over stripping; hierarchical expansion affordances.
design: Flow: agent calls MCP compile_context with ContextSpec(seed_refs=[current session], purpose=continue) + a new prose_with_refs segment profile -> markdown with assistant/human-authored prose verbatim, tool_use/result collapsed to '-> [tool:Bash exit:0 4.2s] pytest ... <ref:action:...>'; new harness session; SessionStart hook injects when POLYLOGUE_REBOOT_FROM=<session_id> marker present (source field startup|resume|clear|compact; don't tax ordinary startups). VERIFY hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext field names against current Claude Code docs. Bundle header instructs: expand any ref via resolve_ref before assuming content. Lineage: session_links inheritance='spawned-fresh' link_type='continuation' new->old via a new record_manual_continuation(child, parent) API; also write the first handoff-kind assertion (kind exists, unwired). Budget rule: prose verbatim to 60% of budget, then oldest prose -> one-line recaps; keep first user message + last N turns verbatim; reuse ContextOmission accounting with reason=budget.

title: Agent context/memory loop (assertions -> preamble -> reboot)
description: The judged-memory loop: agents declare structured claims, the operator judges them, active claims compile into context preambles, and sessions can reboot into compact evidence packs. The substrate exists (assertions, compile_context, compose_context_preamble, SessionStart hook); these children wire the loop closed. Raw-log design criteria (2026-06-29): entries timestamped, expiry metadata, navigable origin refs, restrained injection with expandable indices/refs.

title: Notation overlay: agent-declared structured output -> candidate assertions
description: Agents write markers like DECISION[...]/LESSON[...] in prose; extraction at block enrichment turns them into candidate assertions with evidence refs. Author-declared notation, not heuristic mining — the construct-validity-safe way to get structure out of prose.
design: Marker syntax (operator-recommended default): U+27E6/U+27E7 white square brackets, kind:body inside; verify corpus collision ~0 via polylogue find on the live archive first. Parse point: NOT provider parsers — block enrichment (same layer as paste_spans), over blocks.text where material_origin='assistant_authored' AND block_type='text'; exclude thinking blocks (redacted/absent in exports -> non-deterministic rebuilds) and tool_result text. Kind mapping: decision/lesson/blocker/caveat -> existing AssertionKind; todo/observation/goal/plan -> NOTE with body-prefix subtype exactly like blackboard ([todo] ..., parser archive/blackboard.py:~30 is the precedent). Storage: upsert_notation_assertions in user_write.py modeled on upsert_transform_candidate_assertions (user_write.py:~996-1051): author_kind=agent, author_ref=session:<id>, status=CANDIDATE, context_policy={inject:false, promotion_required:true}, evidence_refs=[message ref]; deterministic id from (session_id, message_id, span_index) -> idempotent re-ingest. SECURITY INVARIANT: notation from ingested foreign sessions stays CANDIDATE — prompt-injection surface (a malicious webpage pasted into a chat could carry markers); the judgment gate is the boundary; preamble compiler must only read ACTIVE claims (it does — ASSERTION_CLAIM_KINDS filter). Hash boundary: spans are derived, must NOT enter session_content_hash (extraction is post-parse; add the hash-invariance test like tests/unit/insights/test_feedback.py). Trap: new AssertionKind/subtype breaks render openapi + cli-output-schemas + the user_audit every-kind-has-a-surface invariant — budget the churn.

title: Defer heavy imports off the CLI startup path
description: ~2s import tax per invocation; also the residual cold cost even with the daemon path absent. Candidates: surfaces/payloads (~2,915 lines of Pydantic model construction), api/archive. Measure first: python -X importtime -c 'from polylogue.cli.click_app import main' | sort by cumulative. Covers the old help-latency and find-select-cold backlog items; add the help-latency devtools budget check as the regression gate.

title: Interactive query latency program
description: Make the front door fast: cold CLI invocations pay ~2s of Python imports; some helps took 5-9s; find-then-select cold spikes; claim-vs-evidence regen 43s. WAL checkpoint + ANALYZE already done (2026-07-03: index.db WAL=0, sqlite_stat1 present, v23). Children are alternative/complementary attacks; CLI->daemon fast path is the structural one.

title: CLI->daemon fast path over UDS (persistent hot process)
description: Route CLI queries through the already-hot daemon process when available: skips import cost, warm SQLite page cache, shared readiness state. Falls back to in-process silently.
design: Health handshake response: {archive_root, index_schema_version, daemon_version, commit, started_at}; client REJECTS on archive_root mismatch (resolve client-side via a leaf path module with no storage imports — check polylogue/paths import graph first). UDS at $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/polylogue/daemon.sock, fallback /tmp/polylogue-$UID/daemon.sock. Server: HTTPServer subclass with address_family=AF_UNIX; client_address is empty string; the disconnect-probe socket peeks work on UDS unchanged. New endpoint POST /api/cli/query: body = normalized root-request param dict (cli/root_request.py output) + format; response = exactly what the CLI would produce (both paths call the same api/archive.py method with the same spec => zero drift). Parity harness: pytest fixture seeds demo corpus, starts daemon on ephemeral UDS, runs N canonical queries both ways, asserts JSON equality (tests/unit/cli/test_daemon_parity.py, default lane).

title: CLI structured-query routing parity with daemon (#1860): no FTS gate for non-FTS queries
description: The daemon discriminates structured-only queries from FTS queries (http.py ~:1789-1793); the CLI calls the search path unconditionally, so structured filters pay the FTS readiness gate. Port the discriminator: branch on spec.query_terms/contains_terms at the single CLI search-vs-list site. Regression: structured-only query on an archive with deliberately-stale FTS must succeed. Verify current state first — v23 + recent work may have changed the shape.

title: devloop-integration --json --check consumed by devloop-review
description: Integration lane has no machine-readable contract; review cannot see branch role, ahead count, stale ledger, replay branches, PR URLs. Add JSON/check mode and have review consume it.

title: Route ops reset --session/--source through the mutation contract
description: Identity resets tombstone directly before the preview/confirmation branch — a typo mutates suppression state without dry-run or JSON evidence. Require dry-run preview + --yes + stable JSON like other destructive ops.

title: Run-projection repository reads: cache-optional source-derived CTEs
description: Terminal query lowerers already synthesize run/observed-event/context-snapshot rows from sessions/blocks, but the relation SQL unconditionally UNIONs cache tables (absent tables still fail) and repository/query-store APIs remain persisted-cache-only. Make terminal relations cache-optional with source-only CTEs + optional enrichment branches.

title: Embedding changed-text full-replace regression vs split embeddings.db metadata
description: Follow-up from the embedding-freshness slice: changed-text reindexing for the same message_id needs an explicit full-replace regression against split embeddings.db metadata (index-tier rows cleared, embeddings tier not).

title: Finish streaming reads: lineage-composed transcripts, messages --full writer, origin-filtered pagination SQL
description: Residue of the streaming-export slice: lineage-composed transcript streaming still falls back to the eager path; read --view messages --full --to file lacks a true writer/iterator renderer; material-origin-filtered message pagination uses the eager fallback until SQL owns the predicate.

title: Attachment byte acquisition for non-inline sources (Drive/zip/local)
description: Acquire bytes where the handle is live (Drive client / open zipfile / allowlisted local path), deposit onto ParsedAttachment.inline_bytes, reuse shipped true-hash write. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Fold agent_forensics.py into polylogue analyze
description: ~70% already materialized. Real gaps: reasoning-token lane on SessionProfile; usage_timeline archive-level insight (tokens/cost per month per model); optional markdown forensics renderer. Then delete the script. Sequenced AFTER the campaign regen (the campaign uses the script one last time). Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Compaction boundary-range columns + effective-context derivation
description: session_events boundary_start/end_position + boundary_message_id; get_effective_context(session, at_position) = what the model actually saw vs full composed prefix. Schema bump + re-ingest. Surfaces: view=effective_context; precise replaced-range signal for stale_context pathology. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Wrap lineage composition reads in a single read transaction
description: Composition uses multiple autocommit SELECTs; concurrent parent re-ingest between reads yields a torn transcript. Hold one deferred read transaction across the recursion (pattern: fts_invariant_snapshot_sync). Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Parser drops: ChatGPT image/asset-only nodes; Antigravity non-UTF-8 bodies
description: chatgpt.py skips before content_blocks are built (image-gen nodes lost); antigravity UnicodeDecodeError bypasses the OSError fallback (whole session dropped). Both silent per-session losses. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Distinguish subagent auto-compaction from main-session acompact
description: agent-acompact-* also fires for Task-subagent self-compaction (~39/187 files <90% overlap; 9 at 0%) — parser assigns wrong parent, composition prepends wrong transcript. Test prefix content/UUID membership before assigning main session as parent. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Preserve uploaded attachment bytes in browser capture
description: chatgpt-dom-v1 captures the attachment chip, not bytes (byte_count=0). Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Live full-ingest catch-up latency + WAL shape
description: 0.2 files/s full-ingest chunks; parse_s ~274s for 50 small files. Recent daemon backoff commits (no-op retry/catch-up chunks, filtered retry paths) address parts — re-measure before working. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Substrate: materialize hook events + OTLP spans into queryable evidence
description: Hook events stored as raw blobs are never materialized (~95% of hook-only signal invisible); OTLP spans likewise. Both converge on the phase-1 write-leg contract. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Substrate phase 2: parse_hermes work-structure fidelity
description: Hermes parser flattens delegation graph/task lifecycle/skills/lineage to flat messages; emit into topology_edges + ObservedEvents. Extension, not invention. Note: operator does not use Hermes yet — value is strategic (sovereign-loop story), not current dogfood. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Substrate phase 1: agent work-event write-leg -> session_events -> read-models
description: record_work_event/emit_decision write leg routed through the existing idempotent ingest seam (no parallel writer); flows into run-projection read models. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Substrate: Polylogue underneath a work-generating agent
description: Invert the relationship: agent emits work to Polylogue live; reads context/evidence back in-loop. OPERATOR GATE: 'a direction, confirm before a large build' (epic wording) — phases below are individually justified, the full program is not yet green-lit. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Provider token accounting complete and auditable
description: Honest cross-provider usage ledger: coverage, caveats, cached-vs-uncached, reasoning tokens, current-window + cumulative. Related: cost-reconciliation probe bead, logical-session cost bead. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Archive schema hygiene for evidence-cockpit read paths
description: Targeted schema-extension/hygiene (unsafe joins, JSON checks, durable-row rules) — not a rewrite. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Contracts own surfaces; delete parallel dispatch
description: Architectural cleanup campaign: make existing contracts (query DSL, terminal units, refs, read-view profiles, action/route contracts, generated docs/schemas) the actual owners of behavior; delete hand-written parallel surfaces. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Assertions: consumer wiring + lifecycle tightening for unified overlays
description: Assertion substrate is the live path; remaining work is consumer wiring + lifecycle (promotion, staleness, expiry) tightening. Unwired kinds exist: handoff, prompt_eval, highlight. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Shell completion as read-only projection of query/AST/field registries
description: Completion/fuzzy-selection/query-builder metadata built on the same grammar+registries used by CLI/MCP/daemon/web — not a second parser. Substantial substrate exists (query_completions tool, projection-unit completions landed 07-03); remaining scope per issue. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: DSL: aggregates beyond count (sum/avg/min/max/percentiles)
description: `group by X | count` is the only aggregate; cost/duration/token questions need sum/avg/percentiles to compose instead of spawning bespoke analyze modes.
design: Pipeline stages are hand-parsed OUTSIDE the Lark grammar (split on |, parsed ~expression.py:1574 and :2777) — no grammar change needed. Chain: 1) add keyword in stage parser; 2) AST dataclass + to_payload (pattern expression.py:312-478), wire into QueryUnitPipelineStage union + pipeline assembly (:511-540); 3) executor mapping in unit_results.py/plan_execution.py; 4) SQL: lower onto per-unit sql_query_method SELECT-list; percentiles nearest-rank in Python over grouped rows (pattern insights/portfolio.py:107-128) — don't fight SQLite; 5) metadata.py aggregate_metrics per QueryUnitDescriptor; 6) shell_completion_values.py _pipeline_completion_context (:290-311); 7) devtools render openapi + cli-output-schemas + cli-reference; explain payloads pick up stages via to_payload — check explain snapshot tests. Line refs are pre-07-03; re-locate before editing.

title: DSL: projection predicates/windows + render/layout stages on attached units
description: `with <units>` now supports all projection units with field selection; next expressiveness step is declared predicates/windows on the attached unit (e.g. with messages[role:user, last:20]) and render/layout stages so read packages and demos are declarable in the query, not in per-view flags. Direction ref: .agent/includes/fables-poly-findings.md High-Value Backlog.

title: Full query DSL substrate (canonical query language owner)
description: The Lark grammar in polylogue/archive/query/expression.py is THE query language; extend in place, sole owner of query semantics. Landed since filing: with-projection for all projection units, field selection for attached units, projection-unit completions. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: Ingest beads issue history as a Polylogue evidence source
description: Beads is Dolt-backed with full history, plus .beads/interactions.jsonl and optional events.jsonl export. That is a work-evidence stream: what was planned, claimed, closed, by which actor, when — exactly the cross-referencing substrate claim-vs-evidence and work-packets want ('agent closed polylogue-42; do the session's tool outcomes support the close reason?'). Design as a normal origin/source (bd export --events as the wire format) through acquire->detect->parse->store; no special-case silo. Also composes with Sinex later (beads events as a Sinex source). Do AFTER the beads workflow has accumulated real history worth querying.

title: Express devloop state in Polylogue substrate (dogfood target)
description: Raw-log 2026-07-03: 'perhaps devloops themselves could be expressed in sinex and/or polylogue and/or beads?'. Beads now owns task state (this migration). The remaining half: focus transitions, handoffs, proof claims, and velocity notes should eventually be archive/assertion data rather than markdown sidecars only — the conductor is a live agent whose sessions the archive already ingests. Candidate first slice: devloop-log dual-writes an assertion (kind=NOTE, author=devloop) with evidence refs to the session that produced the entry. Evaluate against gh-2459 write-leg rather than inventing a parallel writer.

title: Blob store zstd compression (36GB -> est 5-8GB)
description: Content-addressed blobs are uncompressed JSON; zstd frames are self-identifying so no schema change is needed.
design: Address stays SHA-256 of UNCOMPRESSED bytes. No marker column: zstd magic \x28\xB5\x2F\xFD — read path sniffs 4 bytes, falls back to raw. Touchpoints: blob_store.py (write: compress if len>512 and not already zstd; read: sniff+decompress), blob_integrity.py (verify = decompress-then-hash — silently breaks if missed), size accounting (report logical size; add stored_bytes where cheap). Migration: ops maintenance blob-compact [--limit N] — iterate shards, skip magic-prefixed, temp+rename atomic, verify hash before replace, honor pending_blob_refs leases. Level 9 one-shot, level 3 ingest-time. Dependency: zstandard wheel (pure-wheel exists).

title: Bound claim-vs-evidence regen latency (43s on live archive)
description: Regen latency is a caveat on the campaign artifact; likely falls out of the action-unit outcome fields work (SQL-side pairing instead of Python row inspection). Re-measure after cve-fields lands.

title: EQP sweep + dbstat census on a live-archive copy
description: Systematic query-plan audit: monkeypatch sqlite3 execute in a pytest session against a reflink copy (cp --reflink index.db /realm/tmp/eqp-copy.db) logging EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN during a scripted tour of every CLI verb + MCP insight tool; grep for SCAN and USE TEMP B-TREE. dbstat census for table/index sizes (VERIFY dbstat module compiled into nixpkgs sqlite, else sqlite3_analyzer). Never run against the live DB.

title: Provider-token leakage cleanup in public CLI help
description: Public filters use origin vocabulary; some analysis/maintenance help still advertises provider tokens. Tighten help/validation/payload names to origin-only where the contract is public filtering.

title: Output dialect normalization (--format/--json + --to/--out)
description: Mixed --output/--output-format/--format/--to/--out and plain/text/plaintext families across commands; agents cannot infer one rule. Standardize; remove aliases not buying value.

title: Demo/import surface separation (import --demo -> polylogue demo)
description: import --demo --wait --with-overlays mixes demo seeding, convergence wait, daemon scheduling; move demo convergence under polylogue demo, keep import for user paths.

title: Direct `read session:REF` should use read-view semantics
description: Positional read session:... resolves only a ref envelope, unlike find session:... then read --view messages. Route session: refs into the normal read-view request; keep message/block/assertion ref inspection as a named resolver path.

title: Promote facets to a real query-result verb; remove analyze --facets
description: analyze --facets owns the full query-filter stack while top-level facets is narrower; exact equivalence only for a small subset.

title: Refactor analyze boolean mode flags into named projections
description: analyze multiplexes count/facets/cost-outlook/postmortem/portfolio/grouped-stats/diagnostics behind mutually-exclusive booleans; move into explicit projections sharing query relations and render contracts.

title: Collapse read per-view flags into ProjectionSpec/RenderSpec algebra
description: read exposes compact algebra (--projection/--render/--spec) alongside per-view flag clusters (--window-hours, --repo-path, --since-hours, --related-limit...). Extend ProjectionSpec/RenderSpec to cover neighbor/correlation/context options FIRST, then remove aliases. Broad public CLI change, not deletion-only.

title: Stop materializing run-projection cache rows; drop DDL after parity
description: After readers are source-derived: remove readiness/repair debt, reroute relation reads, stop rebuild writes/prunes for session_runs/session_observed_events/session_context_snapshots, then bump schema to drop DDL. Order matters (see Peirce refresh notes in git history of BACKLOG.md).

title: Harden blob hash validation + drop misleading symlink check
description: Defense-in-depth (none currently exploitable): fullmatch 64-hex in blob_path/cleanup_orphans; drop CWD symlink loop in sanitize_path; consider zip aggregate budget. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: topic-pack: staged multi-channel topic-lineage retrieval
description: Flagship retrieval composition: seeds -> signal extraction -> embedding expansion -> time-neighbor -> topology -> classify -> timeline/context-pack/gaps. HARD invariant: never conclude 'no results' from exact-string search alone; precursor recovery via 3 name-independent channels. Full spec: GitHub issue thread (body + comments are part of the spec; this bead is the tracking mirror — improve on the GH body where it is stale).

title: DSL: SEQ modifiers — within:<duration> and {n,} occurrence counts
description: SEQ matches non-contiguous subsequences over ToolCategory with no time/count constraints; add ->[within:5m] and {3,}.
design: Grammar-touching: sequence_step/ARROW region (expression.py:658-666). Matching is Python (runtime_matching.py:66-99): track occurred_at_ms deltas between matched steps — VERIFY the hydrated Action carries a timestamp, else extend build_session_semantic_facts (also memoize it; 3 matchers call it uncached). LALR pitfall: terminal priorities (.4/.6 suffixes) resolve tokenization; any new terminal containing ':' must slot above FIELD_CLAUSE.4 or it is eaten as a field clause.
